metadata.description: Skills and commands for Claude Code and other AI coding agents

plugins.name: blueprint
plugins.description: Three-mode planning system — always-active disposition, explicit blueprint generation (/blueprint), and blueprint execution.

plugins.name: workflow
plugins.description: Push and session commands — conventional commits and session logging.

plugins.name: rust-guide
plugins.description: Opinionated Rust style guide — makes AI-written Rust code look like a human wrote it.

plugins.name: codex
plugins.description: Delegate research, code review, or collaborative multi-turn sparring to Codex CLI. Auto-selects the cheapest model for research and the best available for review and xhigh back-and-forth with Claude.

plugins.name: glm
plugins.description: Delegate to GLM — a fast, cheap model running headless inside Claude Code that beats Opus at frontend. Frontend always goes to GLM; everything else can too. Fast (routers) and regular (models) serving lanes.

plugins.name: distill
plugins.description: Rigorous code refactoring — every line earns its place. Preserves functionality, removes everything that doesn't justify its existence.

plugins.name: parallel
plugins.description: Launch and track parallel work in isolated git worktrees. Prevents duplicate work, tracks active branches.

plugins.name: clog
plugins.description: Teaches Claude to search your Claude Code chat history via the `clog` CLI. Auto-invokes for past-session lookups.

plugins.name: tech-stack
plugins.description: The canonical, opinionated tech stack for new projects — TanStack web, Rust backends, Railway-first, self-hosted data. Always active when scaffolding or choosing technology.

plugins.name: fable
plugins.description: Get the most out of Claude Fable 5 — Fable architects, cheaper models execute. Plans, briefs, and verdicts stay with Fable; normal work delegates to Opus 4.8 and Sonnet 5 subagents. Fable executes only what's hard or high-stakes, or what the user asks it to.
